cit.quote: to do work in brass, make of brass,
arrange, set in order, mend, set
speak to, address, exhort, admonish, blame,
to 'shrink,' contract, shrivel up, wither, pine away,
to slight, treat with contempt, dishonour,
inhabitants of the Isle of Wight
to pay, repay, compensate, yield, restore, reward,
wicker basket with a narrow neck for catching fish,
to come to terms with, be reconciled to,
to make broad, extend, spread, stretch out,
to roast, toast, bake, broil, cook,
to fix the weft, begin the web,
to draw on, allure, seduce, mislead, persuade, instigate,
when a boy, as a youth
to entertain, take care of, attend upon
exequies, last offices to the dead, funeral
to cleave to, adhere, stick to,
to show, display, reveal, disclose, point out,
not ordained, not belonging to an order

usg.expand: attested in poetical texts only, and once only

cit.quote: each one, every one, everything, who or whatever
to have need of, require, want,
it behoves, concerns, belongs, is needful or necessary,
duly appointed member of a guild,
to seek to get hold of,
'hither,' to this side, on this side,
to deliver, let go, release, loosen,
man reduced to slavery by the law
from the beginning to the end
'recapitulatio,' a giving of titles or headings,
to plunder, harry, ravage, devastate, destroy,

usg.expand: attested in poetical texts only, and once only

cit.quote: to bow down, bend, humble, curb, vanquish
to oppose, thwart, be opposed to
join with, make common cause with
single, separate, solitary, private, individual, special,
space of time, time, while, period, season,
for a time, some time, long time
state of being out of the way,
to penetrate, go through, bore, pierce
standard representing a dragon or serpent,
to fix a day for appearance
to believe, trust in, hope, be confident, rely
to search thoroughly, investigate, think out
deprived of, wanting, destitute, barren, sterile,
to hasten, hurry forward, be quick,
what cannot be contended against, inevitable
fine for not detaining an offender,
to exhibit a change of weather
song of praise, canticle, hymn, psalm,
to worry, exasperate, pain, provoke, excite,

usg.expand: attested in poetical texts only, and once only

cit.quote: to draw up, bring up, rear
draw out, pull out, pluck up
lift up, place in an upright position,
to set fire to, ignite, heat, inspire, incite, inflame, burn, consume,
to lull, soothe, set at rest
meanwhile, while, as long as, until
hundred-court, or assembly of the men of a hundred
head of the hundred court, centurion
wretched hole, pit of misery, hell,
to support, sustain, uphold, strengthen, establish,
